title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S8
body:
**Scene 8: 08:30–10:00 · Walter Cooks Dinner for One**

HERO FRAME: A small kitchen, 1949 · a single place setting at a Formica table · a casserole dish on the stove · the radio on the windowsill softly humming · the wall clock showing 7:14 PM

MOTION: kling — steam rises slowly from the casserole. The clock's second hand sweeps. The light through the window shifts from amber to dusk-blue across the meal.

MOOD: The smallest possible domestic gesture against the apocalypse.

AUDIO: Walter: *"I cook for one now. I have always cooked for one. But it feels different. I do not know why it should feel different."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
